gold: Hayır. Son rakam yanlış oldu. 60 değil, 67 olacak sonu. 67 olacak pardon. 68 dediniz siz, 67 ile bitecek.

sigWerErrors.reason: Repeating 'son' (last) does not change the meaning of the sentence, just a minor redundancy.
'Altmış' means 'sixty', and is part of the number 'altmış yedi' (sixty-seven). Inserting it here is necessary for the Turkish number format, so this is not an error.
In Turkish, 'altmış yedi' means 'sixty-seven', so splitting the number into words is equivalent to the numeral. No change in meaning.
Again, 'altmış' is part of the number 'altmış sekiz' (sixty-eight), which is the correct Turkish format. No change in meaning.
Here, 'altmış sekiz' (sixty-eight) is used instead of 'altmış yedi' (sixty-seven). This changes the number being referred to, which is significant in this context.
